reasoning: Mathlib's `RegularSpace` is the pure separation axiom (disjoint neighborhoods of a closed set and a point not in it, no T₁ assumed), exactly matching Munkres's "regular (separation property only)". The nonempty hypothesis `∀ i, Nonempty (X i)` mirrors "Assume each X_α is nonempty" and is genuinely needed (an empty factor makes the product empty and vacuously regular while other factors may fail to be regular). The universal quantification `∀ i, RegularSpace (X i)` correctly captures "then so is X_α", and `{ι : Type*}` is a faithful encoding of an arbitrary index set. Hypotheses, conclusion, types, and quantifier structure all match the informal claim.
